proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with AML, having `feature_025` = 1 is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than having `feature_025` = 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion Wald test for objective_response by each binary feature; effect is response-rate difference feature=1 minus feature=0.
analyses.result_summary: Screened 41 binary features. `feature_025` had response 23.46% when present vs 16.73% when absent, a risk difference of 0.0674 (p=1.00e-300). Cohort response rate was 18.75%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_025` = 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_025` = 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion Wald test; effect is response-rate difference feature=1 minus feature=0.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_025` prevalence was 30.03%. Response was 23.46% for `feature_025` = 1 vs 16.73% for `feature_025` = 0; risk difference 0.0674 (p=1.00e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_015` = 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_015` = 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion Wald test; effect is response-rate difference feature=1 minus feature=0.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_015` prevalence was 40.11%. Response was 22.10% for `feature_015` = 1 vs 16.51% for `feature_015` = 0; risk difference 0.0559 (p=1.00e-300).

analyses.result_summary: `feature_032` prevalence was 9.97%. Response was 16.57% for `feature_032` = 1 vs 18.99% for `feature_032` = 0; risk difference -0.0242 (p=1.43e-5).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A one-standard-deviation increase in continuous `feature_027` is associated with lower `objective_response` probability.

analyses.code: Robust-SE linear probability model objective_response ~ standardized feature.
analyses.result_summary: Linear probability regression gave a per-SD response probability change of -0.0354 for `feature_027` (mean 0.797, SD 0.678; p=1.00e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients in the top quartile of continuous `feature_027` have lower `objective_response` rate than patients below the top quartile.

analyses.code: Two-proportion Wald test comparing feature >= empirical 75th percentile vs below.
analyses.result_summary: Top-quartile threshold for `feature_027` was 1. Response was 16.39% in the top quartile vs 23.10% below; risk difference -0.067 (p=1.00e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients in the top quartile of continuous `feature_031` have lower `objective_response` rate than patients below the top quartile.

analyses.code: Two-proportion Wald test comparing feature >= empirical 75th percentile vs below.
analyses.result_summary: Top-quartile threshold for `feature_031` was 73.3. Response was 16.64% in the top quartile vs 19.46% below; risk difference -0.0282 (p=4.55e-13).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the strongest other binary response-associated features, `feature_025` = 1 remains associated with higher `objective_response` probability.
After adjusting for the strongest other binary response-associated features, `feature_032` = 1 remains associated with lower `objective_response` probability.

analyses.code: Robust-SE multivariable linear probability model with the top positive and negative binary main-effect features.
analyses.result_summary: In a robust linear probability model including `feature_025`, `feature_015`, `feature_023`, `feature_005`, `feature_016`, `feature_032`, `feature_001`, `feature_074`, `feature_003`, `feature_068`, `feature_025` had an adjusted risk difference of 0.0676 (p=1.00e-300).

analyses.code: Robust-SE multivariable linear probability model with the top positive and negative binary main-effect features.
analyses.result_summary: In the same multivariable model, `feature_032` had an adjusted risk difference of -0.0258 (p=4.24e-6).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association between `feature_025` = 1 and `objective_response` is more positive among patients with `feature_015` = 1 than among patients with `feature_015` = 0.

analyses.code: Robust-SE linear probability model with two binary main effects and their product interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Linear probability interaction model `objective_response ~ feature_025 + feature_015 + feature_025:feature_015` estimated an interaction risk-difference contrast of 0.1926 (p=1.00e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association between `feature_025` = 1 and `objective_response` is more positive among patients with `feature_023` = 1 than among patients with `feature_023` = 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the subgroup with `feature_015` = 1, `feature_025` = 1 is associated with a higher `objective_response` rate than `feature_025` = 0.
Within the subgroup with `feature_015` = 0, the response-rate difference for `feature_025` = 1 versus 0 is smaller than in the `feature_015` = 1 subgroup.

analyses.code: Two-proportion Wald test inside the subgroup with the second binary feature present.
analyses.result_summary: Among patients with `feature_015` = 1 (n=20056), response was 34.95% for `feature_025` = 1 vs 16.63% for `feature_025` = 0; subgroup risk difference 0.1832 (p=1.00e-300).

analyses.code: Two-proportion Wald test inside the subgroup with the second binary feature absent.
analyses.result_summary: Among patients with `feature_015` = 0 (n=29944), response was 15.85% for `feature_025` = 1 vs 16.79% for `feature_025` = 0; subgroup risk difference -0.0094 (p=0.0424).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_062` = 1 have a different `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_062` = 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion Wald test; included to document features that did not support a main-effect hypothesis.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_062` showed essentially no response separation: 18.71% when present vs 18.75% when absent; risk difference -0.0004 (p=0.939).
